ogTitle: Lord Howe Island vs Lorn: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lord Howe Island and Lorn.
heading: Lord Howe Island vs Lorn — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lord Howe Island, New South Wales and Lorn, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lord Howe Island has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Lorn (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lord Howe Island or Lorn safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lord Howe Island has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Lorn scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lord Howe Island scores 32/100 for bushfire risk. Lorn scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lord Howe Island or Lorn?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lord Howe Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
